notes: 5 repeated searches after 2 warmup turns; benchmark from scripts/run-benchmarks.sh profile
Added independent TT verification key and collision regression test; no measured node change on profile baseline

Endpoint-row bonus reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted

Increasing the progress bonus above v1 reduced repeated-search throughput; reverted

Lowering bomb and megabomb bonuses reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted
Forward-span bonus reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted

Iterative bomb propagation in recordOne preserved behavior but did not improve the repeated-search baseline; reverted

Added a future-move diversity bonus based on unique legal starts unlocked by the claimed path; profile rose from 339260 to 354475 and short rose to 87822

No measurable change versus baseline; reverted to divisor8

Added a rare-letter tie-breaker favoring paths that claim scarce letters (e.g., the lone 'z' along GLAMORIZE). On the profile scenario total_nodes rose from 364230 to 364270 and deepest_completed_depth stayed 3; effect is tiny but repeatable.
Added a very weak 8-neighbor halo bonus; repeated-search depth collapsed from 3 to 2, so reverting

notes: After implementing the rare-letter tie-breaker, repeated-search totals matched baseline (no throughput or depth change). Keeping tree clean and reverting.
Added a row-band diversity bonus that rewards paths spanning many distinct rows (e.g., GLAMORIZE diagonal vs GLASS horizontal). Repeated-search totals unchanged vs baseline; reverting.
Added a small column-band diversity bonus so diagonals like GLAMORIZE beat one-file paths like EEL when other signals tie. Repeated-search totals reproduced exactly at baseline, so reverting.

Added a frontier mobility bonus from unique neighboring squares around the claimed path (e.g., GLAMORIZE leaves side shoulders; EEL mostly stacks starts on one file). At divisor 6 the repeated-search profile stayed exactly at baseline, so trying a stronger scaling.

Strengthening the frontier neighboring-word-count bonus reordered search in the wrong direction: repeated-search profile fell from 364230 to 345895 while depth stayed 3. Reverting that scaling and trying a less correlated frontier signal.

Switched to a geometry-only frontier count that rewards paths exposing many neighboring squares. It was even noisier: repeated-search profile fell to 340310 with no extra depth, so reverting to the known-good baseline.

Counted distinct future word texts so a lane leaving {stare, stern, stone} would outrank one mostly preserving repeated GLAMORIZE placements. At divisor 4 the repeated-search profile matched the 364230 baseline exactly, so reverting.

Strengthening the distinct future word-text bonus still produced the exact 364230 repeated-search profile total with depth 3. The text-level signal was too correlated with the existing mobility/diversity stack, so reverting.
Rewarded paths whose future follow-ups land on several forward rows instead of one band. That reordered the profile position badly enough to stall at depth 2 on every repeated search, so reverting.
Rewarded paths that preserved many distinct future start squares. That favored noisy multi-anchor clutter over the baseline ordering and every repeated search stalled at depth 2, so reverting.
Restricted the start-square coverage bonus to long follow-ups only, hoping to favor separate long-threat anchors. It still shoved the profile search into the same bad depth-2 ordering, so reverting.

Summed per-square counts of long words along the claimed path so the middle of a GLAMORIZE lane would outrank side squares that mostly feed GLASS. The signal was far too strong: repeated-search depth fell from 3 to 2, so reverting.

Added a small per-square forward-reach bonus based on the average depth of words through each claimed cell, so lanes used by deeper attacks like PERILLED break ties over squares whose words stall locally. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from 364230 to 364570 in two runs, and short held at 87981.

Clipped the existing per-square forward-reach bonus to the best three claimed cells so anchor squares on lanes like PERILLED would outweigh weaker tail cells. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from 364570 to 363575 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Scaled rare-letter credit by each claimed square's forward reach above the board average so the GLAMORIZE 'z' would only matter on deep lanes. The repeated-search profile regressed from 364570 to 360220 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a tiny average forward-reach tie-breaker so words whose whole lane stays active, like PERILLED, could beat paths that only brush one deep anchor. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the 364570 baseline at depth 3, so the signal was too correlated with the existing summed forward-reach bonus and was reverted.

Counted claimed cells whose forward-reach score cleared the board's active-square average plus one, so lanes like PERILLED with several strong interior cells would edge out paths leaning on one GLAMORIZE peak. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the 364570 baseline at depth 3, so the thresholded count was too correlated with the existing summed forward-reach bonus and was reverted.

Added a tiny per-cell bonus for forward reach above the board average so several strong interior PERILLED squares could beat a lane leaning on one GLAMORIZE peak. The repeated-search profile was unstable and collapsed from depth 3 to depth 2 in the last three runs, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a one-point bonus when at least three claimed cells cleared the board average forward reach plus one, so uniformly deep lanes like PERILLED could beat paths that only touch one strong GLAMORIZE anchor. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from 364570 to 360450 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a tiny pair bonus for claimed cells at or above the board-average forward reach, so uniformly deep lanes like PERILLED would gain only when several cells stayed live instead of from one GLAMORIZE anchor alone. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from 364570 to 353380 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a tiny bonus for each claimed cell whose existing forward-reach score already reached row 10+, so lanes with several near-goal interior squares like PERILLED could outrank mixed paths riding one GLAMORIZE anchor. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from 364570 to 360020 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Counted claimed cells whose existing forward-reach score met the board-wide median, so uniformly live lanes like PERILLED could gain from several solid interior cells instead of one GLAMORIZE peak. Two repeated-search profile runs reproduced the same slight regression from 364570 to 364490 at depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Gated each square's raw word-count bonus by its existing forward reach, keeping full credit only for cells at or beyond the board midline and giving shallow cells half credit so GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style deep lanes would beat EEL-style cleanup pockets. The repeated-search profile regressed sharply from 364570 to 292440 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Soft-capped each claimed square's raw word-count bonus at its forward reach plus eight, halving only the crowded excess so deep GLAMORIZE/PERILLED cells would keep nearly full credit while shallow cleanup pockets like EEL lost only their unsupported overflow. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from 364570 to 339295 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Counted only distinct later-in-word neighbor cells that keep moving toward the relevant goal, so PERILLED-style interior squares would keep credit for deeper forks while sideways cleanup pockets like EEL lost non-goalward clutter. Two repeated-search profile runs reproduced the same slight regression from 364570 to 364500 at depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a small per-square bonus for how many long legal words pass through each claimed cell, so GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style anchor lanes would edge out squares whose traffic is mostly short cleanup words like EEL. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from 364570 to 364095 at the same depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a tiny per-square bonus for the average goalward row gain of the next cell in each legal word, so PERILLED-style interior lanes that keep stepping toward the goal would edge out sideways cleanup pockets like EEL. The repeated-search profile came back exactly flat at the baseline 364570 / 72914 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Counted only distinct next cells in each word that stayed non-retreating toward the relevant goal, so PERILLED-style interior squares would keep deeper forks while EEL-style sideways cleanup pockets lost credit. The repeated-search profile still slipped slightly from the kept baseline 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 to 364500 / 72900 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a tiny per-square bonus for how many later letters in each word still stay non-retreating toward the goal, so interior lanes like PERILLED would keep more downstream credit than sideways cleanup pockets like EEL. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the kept baseline 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 to 360240 / 72048 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an experimental policy-table overlay that rescored the top 24 already-good moves using broad static features like path length, forward progress, forward reach, future diversity, and bomb touches. On repeated-search profile it overrode the proven heuristic too aggressively, switching the line to INTERROBANG and collapsing from the kept baseline 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 to 24915 / 4983 / depth 2, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an optional on-disk policy-table stub and let it bubble the top 12 already-good moves upward by at most two slots when their heuristic gap stayed within 24. It still overrode the proven ordering too aggressively, switched the repeated-search line to INTERROBANG, and collapsed from the 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 baseline to 18015 / 3603 / depth 2, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Tightened the optional policy overlay so it only broke exact heuristic ties inside the top-12 prefix, keeping the same broad static features from the cached table. The first four repeated searches stayed at depth 3 but the fifth still slipped to depth 2, leaving the profile summary well below the 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 baseline at 295163 / 59032.6 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a tiny per-square bonus for how many distinct word texts pass through each claimed cell, hoping broad lexical hubs would beat clusters inflated by duplicate path variants of the same word family. The repeated-search profile immediately flipped to INTERROBANG and collapsed from the 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 baseline to 17770 / 3554 / depth 2, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an optional on-disk static policy stub that scored each legal word from broad board-derived features and only broke exact goodness ties during renumbering. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 to 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, but profile-suite regressed driver-sample-a from 298205 to 298045 and slipped the suite average total nodes from 221595 to 221561.67, so reverting.

Added a small per-square bonus for how many long legal words (length >= 8) pass through each claimed cell, so lanes shared by attacks like INTERROBANG or GLAMORIZE would edge out squares whose traffic is mostly short cleanup words like EEL. The repeated-search profile regressed cleanly from the 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 baseline to 364095 / 72819 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Stored the raw versions of the existing diversity and forward-reach signals, then used them only to break exact heuristic ties caused by integer division. On profile the repeated searches improved reproducibly from 364570 / 72914 / depth 3 to 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, and profile-suite improved overall without regressing any board (readme-main 364630, driver-sample-a 298205, driver-sample-b 2010; suite avg_total_nodes 221615.00).

Suite verification for the exact-goodness raw-feature tie-break: readme-main 364630, driver-sample-a 298205, driver-sample-b 2010, suite avg_total_nodes 221615.00 and no board regressed versus the retained baseline.

Added an optional disk-backed policy-weight stub that scored broad per-square averages like forward reach and future diversity, but only as the final tie-break after the retained raw-feature ordering. The repeated-search profile came back exactly flat at 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Cached raw goal-progress and word-length values and used them only after the retained raw diversity/reach tie-breakers, so lanes that actually reached farther would beat same-score paths that stopped earlier. The repeated-search profile came back exactly flat at 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an optional disk-loaded policy-weight stub that attached per-word float scores from broad static features like progress, forward reach, diversity, and bomb access, then used it only after the retained raw diversity tie-breakers. The repeated-search profile came back exactly flat at 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an exact-goodness tie-break that counted distinct future word texts, so three equivalent GLAMORIZE routes would not outrank a lane that kept both GLAMORIZE and STARE alive. The repeated-search profile regressed cleanly from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 360650 / 72130 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an optional disk-loaded static policy overlay that turned broad board features like average forward reach, diversity, and bomb access into a float per-move score, but only as the final tie-break after the retained raw-feature ordering. With WORDBASE_POLICY_WEIGHTS_FILE=src/policy-weights-static.txt, the repeated-search profile came back exactly flat at 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added an optional disk-loaded static policy overlay that attached broad per-move scores from progress, forward reach, diversity, and bomb access, then used it immediately after the base integer heuristic before the retained raw exact-goodness tie-breakers. With WORDBASE_POLICY_WEIGHTS_FILE=src/policy-weights-static.txt, the repeated-search profile stayed exactly flat at 364630 / 72926 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added per-ply killer slots, a player-scoped history table layered after the existing static Wordbase order, and a conservative late-move reduction for late non-TT/non-killer moves. The repeated-search profile collapsed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 8340 / 1668 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a player-specific exact-goodness tie-break that summed the goal progress of distinct preserved follow-up words, so moves keeping deeper continuations like PERILLED-style lanes could beat equally diverse lanes whose surviving words stalled near short pockets like EEL. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Applied diminishing returns to each square's raw legal-word count with an 8-point soft cap plus half-overflow, so GLAMORIZE-style spine cells still scored well but overloaded local clusters stopped scaling linearly. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 339940 / 67988 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Added a final exact-goodness tie-break that preferred higher average per-cell word density when total square traffic was equal, so a uniformly active GLAMORIZE-style lane could beat a route that got the same total from one crowded hub plus dead cells. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Applied a gentler diminishing-returns transform to per-square legal-word counts by keeping counts linear through 16 and then crediting only three quarters of further overflow, so GLAMORIZE-style spine cells still stayed strong while overloaded local hubs lost some duplicate-heavy weight. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 347315 / 69463 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Kept per-square legal-word counts linear through 32 and then credited seven eighths of overflow, aiming to preserve GLAMORIZE-style spines while shaving only a little value from overloaded local hubs. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 353670 / 70734 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Kept per-square legal-word counts linear through 160 and then credited fifteen sixteenths of overflow, so ordinary GLAMORIZE-style spine squares stayed untouched while only the busiest duplicate-heavy hubs lost a sliver of weight. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 360045 / 72009 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Kept per-square legal-word counts linear through 192 and then credited seven eighths of overflow, so standard GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares stayed untouched while only the busiest 200+ duplicate-heavy hubs lost a little weight. The repeated-search profile still slipped from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Applied an extremely conservative diminishing-returns transform that kept per-square legal-word counts linear through 224 and then removed only one point per additional block of 16 overflow words, so normal GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares were unchanged while only the most duplicate-heavy 240+ hubs could lose a couple of points. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Applied a very late diminishing-returns transform only to the heuristic-facing square-word-count bonus, keeping the raw per-path totals for exact tie-breaking while shaving half of any per-square overflow above 224. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Applied a very late diminishing-returns transform only to the heuristic-facing square-word-count bonus, keeping the raw per-path totals for exact tie-breaking while crediting seven eighths of any per-square overflow above 224. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Capped each square's contribution to the heuristic-only square-word-count bonus at 224 while keeping the raw per-path total for exact tie-breaking, aiming to leave GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lanes intact while muting only the most overloaded duplicate-heavy hubs. The repeated-search profile regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364390 / 72878 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep counts linear through 128 and then credit half of further overflow, while preserving the raw path total for exact tie-breaking. That broad taper heavily underweighted strong GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares along with the noisy hubs, and the repeated-search profile regressed sharply from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 346135 / 69227 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep counts linear through 240 and then credit half of any further overflow, while preserving the raw path total for exact tie-breaking. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep counts linear through 224 and then credit three quarters of any further overflow, while preserving the raw path total for exact tie-breaking. That still shaved enough value off the busiest duplicate-heavy hubs to reproduce the same repeated-search regression as the harsher late-overflow variants: profile fell from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square word-count bonus to stay linear through 192 and then subtract a smooth quadratic tail from heavier overflow, so ordinary GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares stayed untouched while only the most duplicate-heavy hubs lost more noticeable credit. The repeated-search profile reproduced the same regression as the harsher late-overflow variants, falling from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to stay linear through 192 and then shave one point per additional block of 16 overflow words, while preserving the raw per-path total for exact tie-breaking. Even that narrow trim still reproduced the same repeated-search regression as the other 192-threshold variants, dropping from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep counts linear through 216 and then credit seven eighths of further overflow, while preserving the raw path total for exact tie-breaking. That still trimmed enough value from heavily reused hub squares to reproduce the same repeated-search regression as the harsher 192- and 224-threshold variants, falling from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Tried a capped additive transform only for the heuristic-facing per-square bonus: each claimed cell kept its raw count plus up to 64 extra points from half the count, aiming to separate cleaner 60- to 120-word lanes without letting 240-word hubs dominate indefinitely. That reweighted the feature far too aggressively, flipping the repeated-search principal variation to INTERROBANG and dropping profile from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 21280 / 4256 / depth 2, so reverting immediately without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to leave each cell linear through 232 and then charge a flat one-point penalty above that, aiming to touch only the most overloaded duplicate-heavy hubs while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares unchanged. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so treating it as flat and reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each cell linear through 240 and then credit only one quarter of further overflow, aiming to touch just the busiest duplicate-heavy hubs while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares untouched. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, so treating it as flat and reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep counts linear through 208 and then shave just one point per additional block of 16 overflow words, aiming to touch only the busiest duplicate-heavy hubs while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares fully intact. The repeated-search profile reproduced the same regression as the harsher late-overflow variants, dropping from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to subtract each claimed cell's count divided by 64, spreading a tiny compression across all busy squares instead of clipping only the single 231-word hotspot on readme-main. The board distribution check showed most live cells sit far below the earlier 192-plus thresholds, but this broader trim still flipped the repeated-search profile back to INTERROBANG and regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 359950 / 71990 / depth 3, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to subtract one point for each additional block of 48 words above 160 on a claimed cell, so ordinary 40- to 160-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares stayed fully linear while genuinely crowded 208-plus hubs lost at most a point. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, including the INTERROBANG principal variation, so treating it as flat and reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to subtract each claimed cell's count divided by 128, aiming for a milder global taper that left ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE-style lane squares nearly linear while trimming one point from genuinely crowded 128-plus hubs. Even this lighter whole-board compression still regressed the repeated-search profile from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364220 / 72844 / depth 3 and switched the principal variation back to INTERROBANG, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each claimed cell linear through 224 and then credit only half of further overflow, aiming to leave ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares untouched while giving the rare 225-plus overlap hub a few points less influence. The repeated-search profile immediately reproduced the same regression as the other hotspot tapers, dropping from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3 and switching the principal variation back to INTERROBANG, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to subtract one point for each additional block of 32 words above 192 on a claimed cell, aiming to keep ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lanes fully linear while lightly compressing the broad high-count tail instead of cutting only the single 231-word hotspot. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, including the INTERROBANG principal variation, so treating it as flat and reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each claimed cell linear through 160 and then credit three quarters of further overflow, so ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares stayed untouched while 200-plus overlap hubs lost a modest slice of their tail value. That broader taper clearly over-compressed busy lanes: the repeated-search profile regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 356060 / 71212 / depth 3 and switched the principal variation back to INTERROBANG, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to cap each claimed cell at 224 words before summing, aiming to leave ordinary GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares linear while preventing a single overcrowded 225-plus overlap hub from dominating move ordering by itself. The repeated-search profile regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364390 / 72878 / depth 3 and switched the principal variation back to INTERROBANG, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to leave each claimed cell linear through 224 and then subtract a flat one point above that threshold, aiming to touch only the known 225-plus overlap hub while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares unchanged. The repeated-search profile was bit-for-bit identical to the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline, including the INTERROBANG principal variation, so treating it as flat and reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep counts linear through 192 and then subtract a tiny quadratic overflow term, so ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lanes stayed untouched while a 231-word overlap knot lost just 2 points instead of dominating on raw local clutter. Even that smooth late-tail compression reproduced the familiar regression from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3 with the same INTERROBANG principal variation, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each claimed cell linear through 192 and then shave one point for every additional 8 words, so ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lanes stayed untouched while a 231-word overlap knot lost only 4 points. Even that gentler taper reproduced the same regression from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 364490 / 72898 / depth 3 with the same INTERROBANG principal variation, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each claimed cell linear through 96 and then credit seven eighths of further overflow, so ordinary 40-word GLAMORIZE-style lane squares stayed untouched while a busier 120-word lane lost just 3 points and 200-plus PERILLED-style junctions lost a little more local clutter credit. The repeated-search profile regressed sharply from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 351630 / 70326 / depth 3 and kept the INTERROBANG principal variation, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each claimed cell linear through 160 and then credit seven eighths of further overflow, so ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares stayed untouched while a 224-word overlap hub lost just 8 points of local clutter credit. The repeated-search profile regressed from the retained 364630 / 72926 / depth 3 baseline to 358255 / 71651 / depth 3 and kept the INTERROBANG principal variation, so reverting without running profile-suite.

Changed only the heuristic-facing per-square bonus to keep each claimed cell linear through 208 and then shave one point for every additional 16 words on that cell, so ordinary 40- to 120-word GLAMORIZE- or PERILLED-style lane squares stayed untouched while moves crossing several 220-plus overlap hubs would pay a small duplicate-clutter tax.
